layer0.description: System-scale moon layout for the Indigo system. Each moon has a fixed orbit band and angle relative to Indigo. This layer is stable and used for macro route planning.

player_start.notes: Player operations begin from a station near Cat's Eye in high orbit.

layer1.description: Scenario-scale destination layer for tutorial routing. Each sublocation belongs to a parent moon and has an approach value from 1 to 10. Approach represents local routing variance relative to the moon rather than literal geometry.

approachScale.1-3: tight and stable local approach; functionally near the parent moon
approachScale.4-6: moderate local variation; destination may differ meaningfully from the moon-level route
approachScale.9-10: high local variance; destination may behave unpredictably relative to its parent moon

buddeDistanceModel.description: Opaque navigation model used by BUDDE for scenario-level distance estimates. Player-facing dialogue may summarize results, but this formula should remain internally consistent.

terms.arcLength: Macro route distance between parent moons derived from layer0 geometry.
terms.orbitChangeModifier: Multiplier representing the cost of rising or dropping between orbit bands during the route. A value of 1.0 means no band change penalty. Larger values increase effective distance.
terms.Normal(0, approach): A normal random value with mean 0 and standard deviation equal to the destination sublocation's approach value.

implementationNotes: Use the destination sublocation's approach value as the standard deviation for the random term.
Apply randomness after macro route and orbit-change cost are computed.
This randomness represents local approach variation, traffic spread, station drift, and destination geometry rather than literal orbital simulation.
Clamp final distance to a minimum value greater than 0.
If deterministic replay is needed, seed the random draw by route, scenario, and tick or contract context.

layer2.description: Scenario-scale destination layer for scenario 2 routing. Each sublocation belongs to a parent moon and has an approach value from 1 to 10. Approach represents local routing variance relative to the moon rather than literal geometry.

approachScale.1-3: tight and stable local approach; functionally near the parent moon
approachScale.4-6: moderate local variation; destination may differ meaningfully from the moon-level route
approachScale.9-10: high local variance; destination may behave unpredictably relative to its parent moon
